title: PS Fachwissen: Rechtsprechung in der späten Kaiserzeit
further_info: Recommended Reading Bodde, Derk und Clarence Morris. Law in Imperial China: Exemplified by 190 Ch’ing Dynasty Cases. Cambridge, MA: Harvard University Press, 1967. Chen, Li und Madleine Zelin. Chinese Law: Knowledge, Practice, and Transformation, 1530s to 1950s. Leiden: Brill, 2015. Chü, T’ung-Tsu. Law and Society in Traditional China. Paris: Mouton, 1965. Head, John W. und Yanping Wang. Law Codes in Dynastic China: A Synopsis of Chinese Legal History in the Thirty Centuries from Zhou to Qing. Durham, NC: Carolina Academic Press, 2005. Hegel, Robert E. und Katherine Carlitz (Hrsg.). Writing and Law in Late Imperial China: Crime, Conflict, and Judgment. Seattle: University of Washington Press, 2007. Huang, Philip C. C. Civil Justice in China: Representation and Practice in the Qing. Stanford: Stanford University Press, 1996. Jones, William C. (Übers.). The Great Qing Code. Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1994. Sommer, Matthew H. Sex, Law, and Society in Late Imperial China. Stanford: Stanford University Press, 2000.
